feedback.text: As a passionate gamer and tech enthusiast, I strongly support incorporating game preservation principles into the Digital Fairness Act. Titles like Satisfactory and Minecraft allow self-hosting, granting players freedom and longevity beyond corporate infrastructure. Ive also experienced firsthand how Plutonium has revived Black Ops 2 through its own dedicated server network, a model that fosters community engagement and prolongs a games life. These examples should not be niche exceptions but a standard for all games. At minimum, every new release should include local server hosting options, or post-official support should default to open server access. Games are more than services, they're cultural artifacts, and players deserve the right to preserve and play them without artificial expiration dates. Lets stop killing games. Lets protect digital ownership and empower users.
